name: Rama
summary: Rama is the prince of Ayodhya and central figure of the Ramayana, revered as an avatar of Vishnu and as the ideal upholder of dharma.

name: Sita
summary: Sita is the princess of Mithila, wife of Rama, and one of the most revered figures of devotion, courage, and dignity in the Ramayana.

name: Hanuman
summary: Hanuman is Rama's devoted ally in the Ramayana, celebrated for strength, humility, wisdom, and complete devotion.

name: Dasharatha
summary: Dasharatha is the king of Ayodhya and father of Rama, Bharata, Lakshmana, and Shatrughna.

name: Bharata
summary: Bharata is Rama's brother, remembered for refusing the throne and ruling Ayodhya only as Rama's custodian.

name: Vishwamitra
summary: Vishwamitra is the sage who asks Dasharatha to send Rama and Lakshmana to protect his yajna, beginning Rama's public journey.

name: Valmiki
summary: Valmiki is traditionally revered as the author of the Ramayana.

name: Shani Dev
summary: Shani Dev is associated with time, discipline, karma, delay, and consequence. Puranic traditions remember him as the son of Surya and Chhaya.

name: Surya Dev
summary: Surya is the solar deity, associated with light, vitality, order, and the movement of time.

name: Chhaya Devi
summary: Chhaya is remembered as the shadow-form connected with Surya and as the mother of Shani in several Puranic traditions.

name: Shiva
summary: Shiva is one of the great deities of Hindu tradition, associated with transformation, tapas, dissolution, and grace.

name: Ayodhya
summary: Ayodhya is the kingdom of Dasharatha and birthplace of Rama in the Ramayana tradition.

name: Mithila
summary: Mithila is the kingdom of Janaka and the setting of Sita's swayamvar.

name: Valmiki Ramayana
summary: The Valmiki Ramayana is the Sanskrit epic narrating the life of Rama across seven kandas.

name: Skanda Purana
summary: The Skanda Purana is one of the major Puranas, containing extensive sacred geography, deity traditions, pilgrimage narratives, and devotional stories.

name: Vishnu
summary: Vishnu is revered as the preserver and protector of cosmic order, remembered through the avatar traditions and many Puranic stories.

name: Krishna
summary: Krishna is a central deity of devotion, wisdom, playfulness, and the Bhagavad Gita.

name: Ganesha
summary: Ganesha is the beloved remover of obstacles and lord of beginnings, wisdom, and auspicious starts.

name: Parvati
summary: Parvati is the mother goddess, consort of Shiva, and source of many Devi traditions including Durga and Annapurna.

name: Lakshmi
summary: Lakshmi is the goddess of prosperity, beauty, auspiciousness, and abundance, closely associated with Vishnu and Diwali.

name: Saraswati
summary: Saraswati is the goddess of learning, speech, music, and wisdom.

name: Subrahmanya
summary: Subrahmanya is the warrior son of Shiva and Parvati, associated with courage, wisdom, and victory.

name: Brahma
summary: Brahma is associated with creation and the unfolding of cosmic order.

name: Indra
summary: Indra is the king of the Devas in many Vedic and Puranic traditions, associated with storms, rain, and divine sovereignty.

name: Varuna
summary: Varuna is associated with waters, cosmic order, and moral law in Vedic traditions.

name: Kubera
summary: Kubera is associated with wealth, guardianship, and Alakapuri, and appears in several Puranic and epic traditions.

name: Kailasa
summary: Kailasa is revered as the abode of Shiva in Hindu tradition.

name: Kishkindha
summary: Kishkindha is the Vanara kingdom connected with Sugriva, Hanuman, and Rama's search for Sita.

name: Dashavatara
summary: The Dashavatara are the ten major avatars of Vishnu, remembered as recurring restorations of dharma.

name: Dasa Mahavidya
summary: The Dasa Mahavidya are ten great wisdom goddesses in Tantric Devi traditions, requiring careful adult/devotional presentation.

name: Durga
summary: Durga is a fierce and compassionate form of Devi, celebrated especially during Navratri and remembered for the restoration of dharma.

name: Kali
summary: Kali is a powerful form of Devi associated with time, transformation, fierce protection, and the destruction of ego and adharma.

name: Lalita Tripurasundari
summary: Lalita Tripurasundari is a central Sri Vidya form of Devi, associated with beauty, sovereignty, wisdom, and the subtle power of consciousness.

name: Matsya
summary: Matsya is Vishnu's fish avatar, remembered for protecting sacred wisdom during a cosmic flood.

name: Kurma
summary: Kurma is Vishnu's tortoise avatar, remembered as the steady support beneath Mount Mandara during the churning of the ocean.

name: Varaha
summary: Varaha is Vishnu's boar avatar, remembered for lifting the Earth from the cosmic waters and restoring stability.

name: Narasimha
summary: Narasimha is Vishnu's man-lion avatar, remembered for protecting Prahlada and breaking the arrogance of tyranny.

name: Vamana
summary: Vamana is Vishnu's dwarf avatar, remembered for revealing how humility can become cosmic power.

name: Parashurama
summary: Parashurama is the axe-bearing avatar of Vishnu, associated with discipline, fierce justice, and the correction of arrogant power.

name: Buddha or Balarama Variant
summary: Some Dashavatara lists include Buddha, while others include Balarama. Khatakshetra should explain the variant clearly instead of hiding it.

name: Kalki
summary: Kalki is the future-facing avatar in many Dashavatara traditions, associated with restoration at the end of decline.

name: Tara
summary: Tara is one of the Dasa Mahavidyas, associated with protective wisdom, crossing danger, and guiding seekers through difficult thresholds.

name: Bhuvaneshwari
summary: Bhuvaneshwari is one of the Dasa Mahavidyas, associated with the vastness of space, sovereignty, and the divine presence pervading the worlds.

name: Bhairavi
summary: Bhairavi is one of the Dasa Mahavidyas, associated with disciplined spiritual intensity, transformation, and fierce devotion.

name: Chhinnamasta
summary: Chhinnamasta is one of the Dasa Mahavidyas, a difficult and symbolic form associated with self-sacrifice, life force, and radical ego-transcendence.

name: Dhumavati
summary: Dhumavati is one of the Dasa Mahavidyas, associated with the wisdom found in loss, old age, emptiness, and what worldly life often avoids.

name: Bagalamukhi
summary: Bagalamukhi is one of the Dasa Mahavidyas, associated with stilling hostile force, speech, and harmful momentum.

name: Matangi
summary: Matangi is one of the Dasa Mahavidyas, associated with speech, music, learning, and forms of wisdom outside ordinary social boundaries.

name: Kamala
summary: Kamala is one of the Dasa Mahavidyas, associated with prosperity, beauty, and the sacred form of abundance.
